front: Describe the generation and detection of ultrasound by a piezoelectric crystal.
back:
• A piezoelectric crystal deforms when a p.d. is applied across it.
  • The crystal oscillates when an alternating p.d. is applied.
  • If this frequency matches the resonant frequency of crystal, ultrasound is generated.
  • Reflected ultrasound in turn causes crystal to resonate.
  • Resonating crystal causes alternating voltage across it.
  • Pulsing is needed as reflected signal needs to be compared to initial signal.
  • With a continuous A.C. signal, comparison is not possible.
  • Backing material damps the crystal vibration after a.c. pulse ends.
  • So that the crystal is ready to receive the reflected signal.
